tools.name: get_guide
tools.description: ⚠️ MANDATORY FIRST STEP - Call this tool BEFORE using any other Canvs tools! Returns comprehensive instructions for creating whiteboards: tool selection strategy, iterative workflow, and examples. Following these instructions ensures correct diagrams.

tools.name: add_elements
tools.description: Manual fallback for creating canvas and adding elements. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Call get_guide first and follow its instructions! Use this tool only when the user's request is not a good fit for Mermaid. If the request can be represented in Mermaid, use add_elements_from_mermaid instead. If room_id is NOT pr

tools.name: update_elements
tools.description: Update elements on a canvas. Requires room_id from a previous Canvs tool result. Pass elements array with id and fields to update. IMPORTANT: in each update object ONLY `id` is required; all other fields are optional patch fields. Include ONLY elements that need changes; elements omitted from the re

tools.name: delete_elements
tools.description: Delete elements from a canvas. Requires room_id from a previous Canvs tool result. Pass ids array of element IDs to delete.

tools.name: query_elements
tools.description: Query elements on a canvas. Requires room_id from a previous Canvs tool result. Returns elements matching optional filters. Use this before update_elements when making small edits to existing diagrams. If no browser has the canvas open, returns an error — ask the user to open the canvas URL in their

tools.name: add_elements_from_mermaid
tools.description: Preferred method for creating diagram elements from Mermaid. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Call get_guide first and follow its instructions! Use this tool for NEW diagrams and LARGE changes to existing diagrams whenever the request can be represented in Mermaid. Prefer translating the request into Mermaid instead o

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_guide" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: ⚠️ MANDATORY FIRST STEP - Call this tool BEFORE using any other Canvs tools! Returns comprehensive instructions for creating whiteboards: tool selection strateg…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "add_elements" is 1976 chars, far longer than this server's median (390). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Manual fallback for creating canvas and adding elements. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Call get_guide first and follow its instructions! Use this tool only when the user's requ…

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: add_elements, query_elements, add_elements_from_mermaid · Public sink: add_elements, add_elements_from_mermaid · Destructive: delete_elements.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: add_elements, query_elements, add_elements_from_mermaid · Public sink: add_elements, add_elements_from_mermaid · Destructive: delete_elements

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
